title: Write C-LARA-2 technical report and academic papers
notes:
Suggestion #8 from admin export (submitted by mannyrayner on 2026-05-06), reprioritized on 2026-05-07 and expanded on 2026-05-09. Prepare a concise initial C-LARA-2 progress report reflecting project status around late May or mid-June 2026, suitable as the source document for related academic publications. See docs/roadmap/reports-and-papers.md for the roadmap and target list. Current prioritized outputs are: (1) a concise Markdown-first progress report by 2026-06-15; (2) a EuroCALL 2026 paper, already accepted, with a 2026-07-31 deadline; (3) an ALTA 2026 target paper, pending final deadline confirmation. Subject to co-author approval, the EuroCALL paper should focus on the user-facing half of the report, while a possible ALTA paper should focus on the implementor-facing half, with some overlap. The report should follow the C-LARA-2 AI-assisted workflow: Codex authorship with humans providing suggestions/review, and optional ChatGPT-5.x critique. A central theme should be that C-LARA-2 is not merely AI-written code: Codex has authored all committed repo content, including code, tests, roadmap documents, issue records, and operational documentation, which may be an important reason it can maintain architectural context over a rapidly growing complex platform. CodePrism currently looks like the closest known comparison point and should be investigated in detail. Priority is P1 because the internal report and accepted EuroCALL paper are active writing targets. On 2026-05-19, suggestion #8 was re-reviewed and treated as an update to existing scope rather than a new issue: publication-planning work already lives in docs/roadmap/reports-and-papers.md and this issue. Priority remains P1. The roadmap is now clarified to treat ALTA 2026 as an explicit target with a mid-September 2026 working deadline and to require that the progress report and downstream papers continue to be produced through the issues-driven Codex workflow, with that methodology stated explicitly in the publications.

Update from human suggestion #21 (submitted 2026-05-25 by mannyrayner): create dedicated infrastructure/workflow for the first progress report due 2026-06-15, including a Markdown-first workspace under docs/publications/progress_report_1 with section-linked top-level outline, planned LaTeX transposition path, and explicit framing of project history, AI-autonomy roles, user-facing functionality, implementor-facing workflow, and issue-grounded future work. This has been incorporated as docs/roadmap/first-progress-report.md plus publication workspace scaffolding.

Update from human suggestion #18 (submitted 2026-05-25 by mannyrayner): extend the first-progress-report roadmap outline with two additional points: (1) under AI role decomposition, explicitly include sysadmin/deployment tasks (notably AWS instance setup directed by Codex and GPT-5); (2) under implementor-facing functionality, explicitly include legacy-project migration work (C-LARA to C-LARA-2 conversion plus server installation/orchestration).

Update from 2026-05-29 Branislav/Cathy discussion summary: revise the initial progress-report framing to prefer a short, selective report over a long catalogue, since much user-facing functionality continues C-LARA. The central theme should be the AI-centered nature of C-LARA-2: the AI writes the committed code, tests, project-management documents, roadmap/issue records, operational documentation, and publication drafts, while humans advise, critique, prioritize, and accept/reject. The report should foreground the resulting speed and flexibility, including the AI-organized AWS migration completed on a days-scale timeline. User-facing examples to emphasize are picture dictionaries (including picture glossing and picture flashcards) and more coherent support for low-resource languages. The report must clearly state that C-LARA-2 is unfinished and identify high-priority future work: stronger AI self-checking/autonomy, mobile deployment/support, and audio recording through the platform, with the latter two especially important for low-resource languages. Also preserve the increasingly likely David Gunkel authorship-paper angle: the initial report may serve as an example of academic writing where the AI has a strong formal authorship claim apart from being human, while treating that as a live research question rather than a settled assertion.

Update from 2026-05-29 roadmap cleanup pass: cleaned docs/roadmap/reports-and-papers.md to distinguish settled framing decisions, current writing targets, planned paper split, related-work comparison, workflow rules, and near-term actions. Corrected the EuroCALL 2026 full-paper deadline to 2026-07-31 and synchronized the reports roadmap with the progress-report Markdown workspace section map.
